01

WHO I AM.

I'm a second-year Computer Science student at BIT Mesra, Jharkhand, with a serious interest in building intelligent systems that actually do something useful.

My work spans training semantic segmentation models on real manufacturing image data at Tata Motors and Tata Steel, to shipping AI-powered web apps with live Render deployments. I care about the gap between "it works in a notebook" and "it works in the world."

Outside of building things, I lead the Robotics Society, manage Akshar Literary Society, and volunteer with Drishti NGO. I believe good engineers show up for more than just code.

WHERE I'VE WORKED.

Tata Steel
JAMSHEDPUR, JH
Data Science Intern
  • End-to-end data processing, model development and evaluation using Python + PyTorch on real manufacturing data.

MAY 2025 — JUL 2025
Tata Motors
JAMSHEDPUR, JH
Data Science Intern
  • Applied ML techniques and Python-based data processing to analyze and preprocess image datasets for downstream model training and evaluation workflows.
  • Implemented a U-Net CNN in PyTorch for pixel-level semantic segmentation; hands-on experience with training pipelines and IoU-based evaluation.
03

THINGS I'VE BUILT.

PROJECT_01
WattWise AI
Full-stack AI tool estimating kWh, CO₂, cloud cost & water usage for AI workloads. Integrated Groq LLaMA 3 for live optimization recommendations. Deployed on Render.

PROJECT_02
SupportBot
Customizable AI customer support chatbot. Multi-turn history, typing indicators, quick-question prompts, topic sidebar. Fully configurable via env variables. Live on Render.
React.jsExpressGroq LLaMA 3Node.js
LIVE DEMO
PROJECT_03
Road Extraction · Satellite Images
U-Net CNN in PyTorch for pixel-level semantic segmentation to extract roads from high-resolution satellite imagery. Full preprocessing pipeline with IoU-based evaluation.
PyTorchU-NetOpenCVIoUPython
VIEW ON GITHUB

PROJECT_04
RAG Teaching Assistant
Converts lecture videos into a searchable knowledge base using Whisper transcription, semantic embeddings, and context-aware LLM prompting to reduce hallucinations.
